MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

Petition For Declaratory Relief And Determination That Proposed Petition For Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Real And Personal Property To Zoya Soane Does Not Violate The No Contest Clauses Of The Family Trust Sub Trusts And Pour Over Will
Off Calendar Sep-29-2008 Continued To Oct-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nathan Paco for Zinaida Martynenko. Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane (Trustee).

Mr. Paco stated that due to some medical problems he had been unable to file a substitution of attorney, but stated he would have it on file by the end of the week. Ms. Erhardt expressed concerns regarding Mr. Paco's standing to represent Ms. Martynenko. Upon the Court's inquiry, Mr. Paco stated he was currently licensed to practice, that he did have written authorization from his client, and noted his client would be filing a supplemental petition with additional causes of action. Ms. Erhardt stated that she would oppose the amendment. The Court instructed Mr. Paco to file his substitution of attorney and instructed Ms. Erhardt to submit the proposed order from the September 15, 2008 hearing for signature. The matter was continued to October 14, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/ R.Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

MINI MINUTES: Matter heard with case #289482 (The Martynenko Family 1991 Trust).
Appearances: Elvira Orly for Zina Martynenko (Surviving Spouse). Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane (Trustee).

The Court stated that there were no oppositions to the Motion to be Relieved as Counsel based on the irreconcilable differences and granted the Motion on both cases. Order will be signed and ready to pickup later today. Ms. Orly to serve the Order and file Proof of Service to that effect. All other Petitions for Declaratory Relief were continued to July 14, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Guitron.

Petition For Declaratory Relief And Determination That Proposed Petition For Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Real And Personal Property To Zoya Soane Does Not Violate The No Contest Clauses Of The Family Trust Sub Trusts And Pour Over Will
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Continued To May-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Called with case #289482 (The Martynenko Family Trust)
Appearances: Virginia Palmer and Elizabeth Erhardt for Zoya Soane. Elvira Orly for the 2nd wife (Zena). Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

In the Trust case:
The Petition for Determination That Proposed Opposition To Zinaida Martynenko's Petition Does Not Violate "No Contest" Clause of The Family Trust and Will was granted. Ms. Palmer presented argument regarding how Probate Code 21320 applies to her petition and would like time to file a brief in support. The Court continued the other petition to December 10, 2007. Ms. Palmer is to file her brief by November 28, 2007. Ms. Orly is to file her response by December 3, 2007.

All the matters in the estate case were continued to May 19, 2008. There will no further actions in the probate matter until the Trust matters are resolved. Status Reports are to be filed two weeks prior. The SFPA will continue to act as the Special Administrator. Ms. Palmer will prepare the orders for both matters.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Mizota.

Petition For Declaratory Relief And Determination That Proposed Petition For Order Under Probate Code Section 850 Authorizing And Directing Transfer Of Real And Personal Property To Zoya Soane Does Not Violate The No Contest Clauses Of The Family Trust Sub Trusts And Pour Over Will
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Continued To Nov-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Line #48 case #289482 (The Martenkenko Trust). Jonathan Ferdon appeared for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Elvira Orly appeared for the second wife. Virginia Palmer appeared for Zoya Soane. Emily Warren appeared for Zinaida Martynenko.

Mr. Ferdon requested to have the letters extended unitl the appointment of the personal representative, which the Court granted. Ms. Palmer informed the Court that there are 3 Petitions for Declaratory Relief on today and 2 on for 11/19/07. She reported that the 2 being heard on the Trust set for 11/19/07 are to be determined first and that the 3 that are scheduled for today can wait until then. Ms Palmer requested that the 2 petitions for probate be continued. The Court indicated that the 1991 will is ready to go and that the other probate matter is still waiting for the Proof of Subscribing Witness.

The Petition To Set Aside Conveyances to Trust is to be taken off calendar. The amended Petition To Determine Revocability of Trust is to remain on calendar and continued to 11/19/07. All petitions were continued to 11/19/07.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Laura Martinez CSR#11332. Bailiff: Rich Lee. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Off Calendar Aug-13-2007 Continued To Sep-19-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elvira Orly and Emily Warren appearing for Zinaida Martynenko; Virginia Palmer and Elizabeth Erhardt appearing for Zoya Soane; Steven Weinkauf appearing for San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

After Ms. Palmer represented that SFPA had no objections, the Court appointed SFPA the Special Administrator with general powers (signing the Order for same in open court). The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to September 19, 2007 for purposes of statute limitations. The Letters of Special Administrator expire on that date.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722; Bailiff: Saephan; Clerks: R. Michael Diles and C. Joy Guandique.
